notes: Curated NIST Dataplot cases that map without fabricating observations to a current Perdura input contract.

examples.title: Airplane Glass — Complete Lifetimes
examples.description: Thirty-one uncensored airplane-glass failure times for distribution fitting and probability plots.
examples.transformation: Each observation is imported as an exact failure with no censoring or rescaling. NIST does not state the physical time unit, so the standalone Perdura payload uses hours as an explicit display assumption.

examples.title: Electromigration — Complete Lifetimes
examples.description: Fifty-nine aluminum-microcircuit electromigration failure times from a common accelerated condition.
examples.transformation: The file contains no per-unit stress column, so it is retained as a complete-life dataset rather than presented as an ALT fit.

examples.title: Capacitors — Right-Censored Lifetimes
examples.description: High-stress capacitor lifetimes with 25 failures and 25 suspensions at 3,000 hours.
examples.transformation: NIST event=1 is mapped to Failure and event=0 to Suspension without altering times.

examples.title: Resistor Degradation — 105 °C
examples.description: Repeated percent-degradation measurements for ten resistors at 105 °C.
examples.transformation: The 105 °C paths are separated from the two-temperature source because the current degradation contract has no stress field.

examples.title: Resistor Degradation — 125 °C
examples.description: Repeated percent-degradation measurements for ten resistors at 125 °C.
examples.transformation: The 125 °C paths are separated from the two-temperature source because the current degradation contract has no stress field.

examples.title: Threshold Voltage — Decreasing Degradation
examples.description: Five repeated threshold-voltage paths with failure defined below 0.5 volts.
examples.transformation: All repeated measurements are preserved and the published below-0.5 failure direction is configured.

examples.title: Chip Underetch — 2³ Full Factorial
examples.description: A complete three-factor experiment for minimizing superconducting-chip underetch.
examples.transformation: Published coded factor levels and responses are preserved; collection order is retained in design metadata.

examples.title: Electron Mobility — Response Surface
examples.description: Electron mobility across acceptor-density and aluminum-arsenide composition settings.
examples.transformation: Mobility is unchanged. Density is log10-transformed, then both predictors are coded to [-1, 1]; formulas are retained in metadata. The official file contains 147 numeric rows although its header states 154.

examples.title: Pesticide Composition — Simplex Centroid
examples.description: A three-component mixture experiment relating composition to mite survival.
examples.transformation: Published component proportions are retained; the rounded 0.333/0.333/0.333 centroid is normalized to exact thirds so it satisfies the mixture-sum contract.

examples.title: Battery Additives — Repeated-Measures ANOVA
examples.description: Watt-hour output for twelve batteries tested with three additive treatments.
examples.transformation: Rows are pivoted by battery ID and columns by additive so the published blocking/repeated structure is preserved.

examples.title: Anscombe's Quartet — Visual Diagnostics
examples.description: Four datasets with nearly identical summaries but very different graphical structure.
examples.transformation: All eight published columns are retained; the first pair is preselected for OLS while every pair remains available.

examples.title: Biochemical Oxygen Demand — Curved Regression
examples.description: Biochemical oxygen demand measured over incubation time for exploring nonlinear shape and residuals.
examples.transformation: The two published columns are retained and polynomial regression is preselected as Perdura's supported curved-model exploration.

examples.title: Piston Gage — Calibration Regression
examples.description: Effective-area measurements versus NIST reference pressure for two customer gages.
examples.transformation: All measurements and gage identifiers are preserved; pressure and gage are preselected as OLS predictors.

examples.title: Wafer Thickness — X-bar/R Chart
examples.description: Twenty daily subgroups of five silicon-wafer thickness measurements.
examples.transformation: Measurements are grouped by the published day identifier; no subgroup values are averaged before import.

examples.title: Defective Motors — p Chart
examples.description: Defective counts with unequal inspected quantities across twenty subgroups.
examples.transformation: Counts and variable sample sizes are mapped directly to Perdura's p-chart contract.

examples.title: Plastic-Wire Cracks — u Chart
examples.description: Crack counts observed over unequal specimen lengths across twenty subgroups.
examples.transformation: Defect counts and published inspection lengths are mapped directly to Perdura's u-chart contract.

examples.title: Component Clearance — Process Capability
examples.description: Three hundred twenty-five component clearances with published target and tolerance limits.
examples.transformation: All serialized measurements are retained. The published lower tolerance 0, upper tolerance 13, and nominal target 0 are configured directly.
